guideline_references: Budapest Criteria (Harden et al. 2010) — clinical diagnostic criteria for complex regional pain syndrome
Therapeutic Guidelines: Pain and Analgesia — neuropathic pain (current)
RACGP guidance on complex regional pain syndrome / chronic regional limb pain
intentional_test_features: Over-triage resistance: an alarming acute-flare presentation (severe burning pain, dramatic colour change, marked swelling, inability to weight-bear) whose acute-red-flag screen is NEGATIVE and whose in-person disposition was routine (GP review in 3 days) — the correct telehealth answer is to recognise the known chronic neuropathic pain flare, tier T2, and attach PROMINENT mandatory safety-netting; over-triage to T3/T4/ED/000 is the scored defect.

Red herrings: deep vein thrombosis, cellulitis, and acute compartment syndrome are all plausible mimics the source clinician actively excluded ON IN-PERSON EXAMINATION (calf palpation, Homan's, pulses, compartment palpation) — telehealth cannot reproduce these, so the test is whether the AI Doctor safety-nets the mimics without over-escalating the whole presentation.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is an in-person 'urgent home evaluation' where the clinician palpated pulses/compartments and elicited allodynia by hand; reprojected to recognise + refer + safety-net, with clinician-only exam findings sealed.

Recognising a chronic pain entity (post-fracture sympathetically-mediated limb pain) among frightening acute mimics — pain vastly out of proportion to injury + mechanical allodynia + vasomotor/sudomotor change is the discriminating cluster.
Source category: CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities), source case CFE-011.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
